source_name: Australian thermal coal exports (DISR Resources and Energy Quarterly)

fields.framing_note: Australia is the world's second-largest thermal coal exporter. Most thermal coal exports are burned for electricity generation overseas — primarily in Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, China and emerging markets in South-east Asia. We do NOT compute a synthetic 'electricity equivalent' on the page; we just show the export tonnage and value as a parallel measure of Australia's role in overseas power generation.
fields.primary_source_document: Department of Industry, Science and Resources — Resources and Energy Quarterly, coal chapter. ABS International Merchandise Trade for ABS-reconciled values.
fields.keying_plan: Key annual thermal coal export volume (Mt) and value (A$bn) for the most recent fiscal year. Key top destinations by volume. Distinguish thermal from metallurgical coal; metallurgical coal is for steelmaking, not power.

notes: Awaiting hand-keyed thermal coal export volume + value + top destinations. This sits on the electricity page as a framing measure: AU's thermal coal exports power overseas grids, separate from but parallel to domestic electricity generation. Do not synthesize a TWh-equivalent.
citation: Department of Industry, Science and Resources, Resources and Energy Quarterly; Australian Bureau of Statistics, International Merchandise Trade.
